TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: uzai a vuluvulung a tsautsau a navaik a qemaɫup.
S.PHON: uzai a vuɭuvuɭuŋ a ʦauʦau a navaik a qəmaʎup.
S.FORM: uzai a vuluvulung a caucau a navaik a qemaljup.
S.PHON: uzai a vuɣuvuɣuŋ a ʦauʦau a navaik a qəmaʎup.
S.TRANSL: There was an old person who went hunting.

S.FORM: manu djemumak tua vetsiar a bulabulay.
S.PHON: manu ɉəmumak tua vəʦiar a buɭabuɭaj.
S.FORM: manu djemumak tua veciar a bulabulay.
S.PHON: manu ɉəmumak tua vəʦiaR a buɣabuɣaj.
S.TRANSL: He found some beautiful flowers,

S.FORM: kiyayain tu sipavai tua marka aɫak a vavayavayan.
S.PHON: kijajain tu sipavai tua marka aʎak a vavajavajan.
S.FORM: kiyayain tu sipavai tua marka aljak a vavayavayan.
S.PHON: kijajain tu sipavai tua maRka aʎak a vavajavajan.
S.TRANSL: and picked them to give to his daughters.

S.FORM: manu nua qatjuvi a vetsiar a kiniyaya.
S.PHON: manu nua qacuvi a vəʦiar a kinijaja.
S.FORM: manu nua qatjuvi a veciar a kiniyaya.
S.PHON: manu nua qaʦuvi a vəʦiaR a kinijaja.
S.TRANSL: The flowers he picked belonged to a snake.

S.FORM: qau mangtjez a qatjuvi.
S.PHON: qau maŋcəz a qacuvi.
S.FORM: qau mangtjez a qatjuvi.
S.PHON: qau maŋʦəz a qaʦuvi.
S.TRANSL: The snake came along.

S.FORM: "aku su kiyayain a ku vetsiar?
S.PHON: "aku su kijajain a ku vəʦiar
S.FORM: "aku su kiyayain a ku veciar?
S.PHON: "aku su kijajain a ku vəʦiaR
S.TRANSL: “Why have you picked my flowers?

S.FORM: patsikelu.
S.PHON: paʦikəɭu.
S.FORM: pacikelu.
S.PHON: paʦikəɣu.
S.TRANSL: Put them back.

S.FORM: nu ini su patsikeli, ku katsi sun," ayain nua qatjuvi a vuluvulung.
S.PHON: nu ini su paʦikəɭi, ku kaʦi sun," ajain nua qacuvi a vuɭuvuɭuŋ.
S.FORM: nu ini su pacikeli, ku kaci sun," ayain nua qatjuvi a vuluvulung.
S.PHON: nu ini su paʦikəɣi, ku kaʦi sun," ajain nua qaʦuvi a vuɣuvuɣuŋ.
S.TRANSL: If you don’t put them back, I’ll bite you,” said the snake to the old man.

S.FORM: qau patsikelen pasa tjua qapulu azua vetsiar.
S.PHON: qau paʦikəɭən pasa cua qapuɭu azua vəʦiar.
S.FORM: qau pacikelen pasa tjua qapulu azua veciar.
S.PHON: qau paʦikəɣən pasa ʦua qapuɣu azua vəʦiaR.
S.TRANSL: So he put the flowers back to the roots.

S.FORM: ɫakua ini anga a patsikel.
S.PHON: ʎakua ini aŋa a paʦikəɭ.
S.FORM: ljakua ini anga a pacikel.
S.PHON: ʎakua ini aŋa a paʦikəɣ.
S.TRANSL: But he couldn’t really put them back.

S.FORM: nu patsikelen, matjanitjani.
S.PHON: nu paʦikəɭən, macanicani.
S.FORM: nu pacikelen, matjanitjani.
S.PHON: nu paʦikəɣən, maʦaniʦani.
S.TRANSL: When he put them back, they fell over.

S.FORM: kivadaq a qatjuvi: "uzai a nisun a aɫak a vavayan, kemuda?"
S.PHON: kivadaq a qacuvi: "uzai a nisun a aʎak a vavajan, kəmuda"
S.FORM: kivadaq a qatjuvi: "uzai a nisun a aljak a vavayan, kemuda?"
S.PHON: kivadaq a qaʦuvi: "uzai a nisun a aʎak a vavajan, kəmuda"
S.TRANSL: The snake asked: “Have you got a daughter by any chance?”

S.FORM: "uzai," aya vuluvulung.
S.PHON: "uzai," aja vuɭuvuɭuŋ.
S.FORM: "uzai," aya vuluvulung.
S.PHON: "uzai," aja vuɣuvuɣuŋ.
S.TRANSL: “I have,” said the old man.

S.FORM: "nu maitazua, katsuu azua vetsiar, sa pavayan tua su aɫak.
S.PHON: "nu maitazua, kaʦuu azua vəʦiar, sa pavajan tua su aʎak.
S.FORM: "nu maitazua, kacuu azua veciar, sa pavayan tua su aljak.
S.PHON: "nu maitazua, kaʦuu azua vəʦiaR, sa pavajan tua su aʎak.
S.TRANSL: “In that case, take the flowers and offer them to your children.

S.FORM: a tjenglay tazua vetsiar niaken anga a vaɫaw.
S.PHON: a cəŋɭaj tazua vəʦiar niakən aŋa a vaʎaw.
S.FORM: a tjenglay tazua veciar niaken anga a valjaw.
S.PHON: a ʦəŋɣaj tazua vəʦiaR niakən aŋa a vaʎaw.
S.TRANSL: The one who likes the flowers will be my wife.

S.FORM: nu ini su papuvaɫavan tjanuaken, ku katsi sun," ayain a vuluvulung.
S.PHON: nu ini su papuvaʎavan canuakən, ku kaʦi sun," ajain a vuɭuvuɭuŋ.
S.FORM: nu ini su papuvaljavan tjanuaken, ku kaci sun," ayain a vuluvulung.
S.PHON: nu ini su papuvaʎavan ʦanuakən, ku kaʦi sun," ajain a vuɣuvuɣuŋ.
S.TRANSL: If you don’t give her to me as my wife, I’ll bite you,” he said to the old man.

S.FORM: qau katsuin nua vuluvulung azua vetsiar a pasa tjumaq; sa kivadaqi a aɫak tu tima a tjenglay tazua vetsiar.
S.PHON: qau kaʦuin nua vuɭuvuɭuŋ azua vəʦiar a pasa cumaq; sa kivadaqi a aʎak tu tima a cəŋɭaj tazua vəʦiar.
S.FORM: qau kacuin nua vuluvulung azua veciar a pasa tjumaq; sa kivadaqi a aljak tu tima a tjenglay tazua veciar.
S.PHON: qau kaʦuin nua vuɣuvuɣuŋ azua vəʦiaR a pasa ʦumaq; sa kivadaqi a aʎak tu tima a ʦəŋɣaj tazua vəʦiaR.
S.TRANSL: The old man took the flowers home, and asked his children which of them liked the flowers.

S.FORM: manu masengats a tjaɫavuluvulungan a vavayan.
S.PHON: manu masəŋaʦ a caʎavuɭuvuɭuŋan a vavajan.
S.FORM: manu masengac a tjaljavuluvulungan a vavayan.
S.PHON: manu masəŋaʦ a ʦaʎavuɣuvuɣuŋan a vavajan.
S.TRANSL: The oldest daughter didn’t like them at all.

S.FORM: qau qemaung azua vuluvulung.
S.PHON: qau qəmauŋ azua vuɭuvuɭuŋ.
S.FORM: qau qemaung azua vuluvulung.
S.PHON: qau qəmauŋ azua vuɣuvuɣuŋ.
S.TRANSL: The old man cried.

S.FORM: "nu masengats mun, muri katsen aken nua qatjuvi," aya tjemumaɫ tua marka aɫak.
S.PHON: "nu masəŋaʦ mun, muri kaʦən akən nua qacuvi," aja cəmumaʎ tua marka aʎak.
S.FORM: "nu masengac mun, muri kacen aken nua qatjuvi," aya tjemumalj tua marka aljak.
S.PHON: "nu masəŋaʦ mun, muRi kaʦən akən nua qaʦuvi," aja ʦəmumaʎ tua maRka aʎak.
S.TRANSL: “If you all dislike them, I’ll be bitten by a snake,” he told his children.

S.FORM: qau penaula a sipuɫaɫakan a vavayan.
S.PHON: qau pənauɭa a sipuʎaʎakan a vavajan.
S.FORM: qau penaula a sipuljaljakan a vavayan.
S.PHON: qau pənauɣa a sipuʎaʎakan a vavajan.
S.TRANSL: His youngest daughter had pity on him.

S.FORM: "nu maitazua, idan azua vetsiar tjanuaken anga.
S.PHON: "nu maitazua, idan azua vəʦiar canuakən aŋa.
S.FORM: "nu maitazua, idan azua veciar tjanuaken anga.
S.PHON: "nu maitazua, idan azua vəʦiaR ʦanuakən aŋa.
S.TRANSL: “In that case, give the flowers to me.

S.FORM: puvaɫaw aken tua qatjuvi," aya.
S.PHON: puvaʎaw akən tua qacuvi," aja.
S.FORM: puvaljaw aken tua qatjuvi," aya.
S.PHON: puvaʎaw akən tua qaʦuvi," aja.
S.TRANSL: I’ll marry the snake,” she said.

S.FORM: sipavai nua vuluvulung.
S.PHON: sipavai nua vuɭuvuɭuŋ.
S.FORM: sipavai nua vuluvulung.
S.PHON: sipavai nua vuɣuvuɣuŋ.
S.TRANSL: So the old man gave them to her.

S.FORM: qau ka tjelutjeluan mangtjez a qatjuvi.
S.PHON: qau ka cəɭucəɭuan maŋcəz a qacuvi.
S.FORM: qau ka tjelutjeluan mangtjez a qatjuvi.
S.PHON: qau ka ʦəɣuʦəɣuan maŋʦəz a qaʦuvi.
S.TRANSL: On the third day the snake came.

S.FORM: "inu anga niaken a vaɫaw?" aya a qatjuvi.
S.PHON: "inu aŋa niakən a vaʎaw" aja a qacuvi.
S.FORM: "inu anga niaken a valjaw?" aya a qatjuvi.
S.PHON: "inu aŋa niakən a vaʎaw" aja a qaʦuvi.
S.TRANSL: “Where is my wife?” said the snake.

S.FORM: qau situlek nua kama.
S.PHON: qau situɭək nua kama.
S.FORM: qau situlek nua kama.
S.PHON: qau situɣək nua kama.
S.TRANSL: The father pointed her out.

S.FORM: qau katsuin azua vavayan nua qatjuvi a sema tjua tapaw nimadju.
S.PHON: qau kaʦuin azua vavajan nua qacuvi a səma cua tapaw nimaɉu.
S.FORM: qau kacuin azua vavayan nua qatjuvi a sema tjua tapaw nimadju.
S.PHON: qau kaʦuin azua vavajan nua qaʦuvi a səma ʦua tapaw nimaɉu.
S.TRANSL: So the snake took the girl to his house.

S.FORM: manu vaivaik tiamadju a maḍusa i djalan.
S.PHON: manu vaivaik tiamaɉu a maɖusa i ɉaɭan.
S.FORM: manu vaivaik tiamadju a madrusa i djalan.
S.PHON: manu vaivaik tiamaɉu a maɖusa i ɉaɣan.
S.TRANSL: The two of them were going along the path;

S.FORM: mintu mapavalit tu tsautsau azua qatjuvi tu vangsangsar.
S.PHON: mintu mapavaɭit tu ʦauʦau azua qacuvi tu vaŋsaŋsar.
S.FORM: mintu mapavalit tu caucau azua qatjuvi tu vangsangsar.
S.PHON: mintu mapavaɣit tu ʦauʦau azua qaʦuvi tu vaŋsaŋsaR.
S.TRANSL: and then the snake changed into a handsome man.

S.FORM: qau masalu aravats azua vavayan.
S.PHON: qau masaɭu aravaʦ azua vavajan.
S.FORM: qau masalu aravac azua vavayan.
S.PHON: qau masaɣu aRavaʦ azua vavajan.
S.TRANSL: The girl was very pleased.

S.FORM: djemaɫun tiamadju a maḍusa itjua tapaw nua qatjuvi.
S.PHON: ɉəmaʎun tiamaɉu a maɖusa icua tapaw nua qacuvi.
S.FORM: djemaljun tiamadju a madrusa itjua tapaw nua qatjuvi.
S.PHON: ɉəmaʎun tiamaɉu a maɖusa iʦua tapaw nua qaʦuvi.
S.TRANSL: The two of them reached the snake’s house.

S.FORM: mintu bulay aravats azua tapaw.
S.PHON: mintu buɭaj aravaʦ azua tapaw.
S.FORM: mintu bulay aravac azua tapaw.
S.PHON: mintu buɣaj aRavaʦ azua tapaw.
S.TRANSL: The house was very fine,

S.FORM: qaiɫungan a patsatsikel.
S.PHON: qaiʎuŋan a paʦaʦikəɭ.
S.FORM: qailjungan a pacacikel.
S.PHON: qaiʎuŋan a paʦaʦikəɣ.
S.TRANSL: with mirrors all round.

S.FORM: nu kidjekets a lalangaw, seḍelas tua kinabulay nua tapaw.
S.PHON: nu kiɉəkəʦ a ɭaɭaŋaw, səɖəɭas tua kinabuɭaj nua tapaw.
S.FORM: nu kidjekec a lalangaw, sedrelas tua kinabulay nua tapaw.
S.PHON: nu kiɉəkəʦ a ɣaɣaŋaw, səɖəɣas tua kinabuɣaj nua tapaw.
S.TRANSL: When flies tried to alight on it, they slipped because it was so fine.

S.FORM: qau azua qatjuvi ini paɫetsasatsasavi azua vaɫaw; marekutj tu maqunvuɫan.
S.PHON: qau azua qacuvi ini paʎəʦasaʦasavi azua vaʎaw; marəkuc tu maqunvuʎan.
S.FORM: qau azua qatjuvi ini paljecasacasavi azua valjaw; marekutj tu maqunvuljan.
S.PHON: qau azua qaʦuvi ini paʎəʦasaʦasavi azua vaʎaw; maRəkuʦ tu maqunvuʎan.
S.TRANSL: The snake wouldn’t let his wife go outside. He was afraid of her (or the house) getting dirty.

S.FORM: manu vaik azua qatjuvi a sema kavuavuan.
S.PHON: manu vaik azua qacuvi a səma kavuavuan.
S.FORM: manu vaik azua qatjuvi a sema kavuavuan.
S.PHON: manu vaik azua qaʦuvi a səma kavuavuan.
S.TRANSL: The snake went off to the fields.

S.FORM: mangtjez azua kaka a tjaɫavuluvulungan a vavayan.
S.PHON: maŋcəz azua kaka a caʎavuɭuvuɭuŋan a vavajan.
S.FORM: mangtjez azua kaka a tjaljavuluvulungan a vavayan.
S.PHON: maŋʦəz azua kaka a ʦaʎavuɣuvuɣuŋan a vavajan.
S.TRANSL: His wife’s oldest sister came.

S.FORM: "aku bulay aravats a tsu a tapaw?
S.PHON: "aku buɭaj aravaʦ a ʦu a tapaw
S.FORM: "aku bulay aravac a cu a tapaw?
S.PHON: "aku buɣaj aRavaʦ a ʦu a tapaw
S.TRANSL: “Why is the house so fine?

S.FORM: kana tiaken a napuvaɫaw," aya timadju a saɫinga.
S.PHON: kana tiakən a napuvaʎaw," aja timaɉu a saʎiŋa.
S.FORM: kana tiaken a napuvaljaw," aya timadju a saljinga.
S.PHON: kana tiakən a napuvaʎaw," aja timaɉu a saʎiŋa.
S.TRANSL: If only I had married him,” she said in her longing for it.

S.FORM: qau "nangaq a ku qetsin tsu a vaɫaw nua qatjuvi," aya timadju a kinemnem.
S.PHON: qau "naŋaq a ku qəʦin ʦu a vaʎaw nua qacuvi," aja timaɉu a kinəmnəm.
S.FORM: qau "nangaq a ku qecin cu a valjaw nua qatjuvi," aya timadju a kinemnem.
S.PHON: qau "naŋaq a ku qəʦin ʦu a vaʎaw nua qaʦuvi," aja timaɉu a kinəmnəm.
S.TRANSL: “I must kill the snake’s wife,” she thought.

S.FORM: kiɫavaran a kaka tjaɫavuluvulungan: "nangaq a kiqaiɫungan itjen, tima tjabulay itjen."
S.PHON: kiʎavaran a kaka caʎavuɭuvuɭuŋan: "naŋaq a kiqaiʎuŋan icən, tima cabuɭaj icən."
S.FORM: kiljavaran a kaka tjaljavuluvulungan: "nangaq a kiqailjungan itjen, tima tjabulay itjen."
S.PHON: kiʎavaRan a kaka ʦaʎavuɣuvuɣuŋan: "naŋaq a kiqaiʎuŋan iʦən, tima ʦabuɣaj iʦən."
S.TRANSL: The oldest sister said: “Let’s look in the mirror, to see who is more beautiful.”

S.FORM: qau kiqaiɫungan tiamadju maḍusa.
S.PHON: qau kiqaiʎuŋan tiamaɉu maɖusa.
S.FORM: qau kiqailjungan tiamadju madrusa.
S.PHON: qau kiqaiʎuŋan tiamaɉu maɖusa.
S.TRANSL: The two of them looked in the mirror,

S.FORM: manu tjabulay azua vaɫaw nua qatjuvi.
S.PHON: manu cabuɭaj azua vaʎaw nua qacuvi.
S.FORM: manu tjabulay azua valjaw nua qatjuvi.
S.PHON: manu ʦabuɣaj azua vaʎaw nua qaʦuvi.
S.TRANSL: and the snake’s wife was more beautiful.

S.FORM: qau ini kasalu azua tjaɫavuluvulungan.
S.PHON: qau ini kasaɭu azua caʎavuɭuvuɭuŋan.
S.FORM: qau ini kasalu azua tjaljavuluvulungan.
S.PHON: qau ini kasaɣu azua ʦaʎavuɣuvuɣuŋan.
S.TRANSL: The oldest sister was not convinced.

S.FORM: "djaɫepan tu namitung sun tua bulabulay, avan su sikabulay," aya.
S.PHON: "ɉaʎəpan tu namituŋ sun tua buɭabuɭaj, avan su sikabuɭaj," aja.
S.FORM: "djaljepan tu namitung sun tua bulabulay, avan su sikabulay," aya.
S.PHON: "ɉaʎəpan tu namituŋ sun tua buɣabuɣaj, avan su sikabuɣaj," aja.
S.TRANSL: “Of course it’s because you’ve got a fine robe on; that’s why you look nice,” she said.

S.FORM: qau pavalivaliten a itung, sa kiqaiɫungan.
S.PHON: qau pavaɭivaɭitən a ituŋ, sa kiqaiʎuŋan.
S.FORM: qau pavalivaliten a itung, sa kiqailjungan.
S.PHON: qau pavaɣivaɣitən a ituŋ, sa kiqaiʎuŋan.
S.TRANSL: So they swopped robes and looked in the mirror.

S.FORM: ɫakua tjabulay angata azua vaɫaw nua qatjuvi.
S.PHON: ʎakua cabuɭaj aŋata azua vaʎaw nua qacuvi.
S.FORM: ljakua tjabulay angata azua valjaw nua qatjuvi.
S.PHON: ʎakua ʦabuɣaj aŋata azua vaʎaw nua qaʦuvi.
S.TRANSL: But the snake’s wife was definitely more beautiful.

S.FORM: kiɫavaran anan uta azua tjavulung a vavayan: "nangaq a vaik itjen a kiqaiɫungan a sema tjua laku," ayain azua vaɫaw nua qatjuvi.
S.PHON: kiʎavaran anan uta azua cavuɭuŋ a vavajan: "naŋaq a vaik icən a kiqaiʎuŋan a səma cua ɭaku," ajain azua vaʎaw nua qacuvi.
S.FORM: kiljavaran anan uta azua tjavulung a vavayan: "nangaq a vaik itjen a kiqailjungan a sema tjua laku," ayain azua valjaw nua qatjuvi.
S.PHON: kiʎavaRan anan uta azua ʦavuɣuŋ a vavajan: "naŋaq a vaik iʦən a kiqaiʎuŋan a səma ʦua ɣaku," ajain azua vaʎaw nua qaʦuvi.
S.TRANSL: The older said once more: “Let’s go to the well to look at our reflections,” she said to the snake’s wife.

S.FORM: qau vaik tiamadju a kiqaiɫungan a sema tjua laku.
S.PHON: qau vaik tiamaɉu a kiqaiʎuŋan a səma cua ɭaku.
S.FORM: qau vaik tiamadju a kiqailjungan a sema tjua laku.
S.PHON: qau vaik tiamaɉu a kiqaiʎuŋan a səma ʦua ɣaku.
S.TRANSL: They went to the well to look at their reflections.

S.FORM: ɫakua tjabulay angata azua vaɫaw nua qatjuvi.
S.PHON: ʎakua cabuɭaj aŋata azua vaʎaw nua qacuvi.
S.FORM: ljakua tjabulay angata azua valjaw nua qatjuvi.
S.PHON: ʎakua ʦabuɣaj aŋata azua vaʎaw nua qaʦuvi.
S.TRANSL: But the snake’s wife was definitely more beautiful.

S.FORM: limutseng azua tjavulung a vavayan.
S.PHON: ɭimuʦəŋ azua cavuɭuŋ a vavajan.
S.FORM: limuceng azua tjavulung a vavayan.
S.PHON: ɣimuʦəŋ azua ʦavuɣuŋ a vavajan.
S.TRANSL: The older girl was angry.

S.FORM: sizurung a paɫetaladj tua laku.
S.PHON: sizuruŋ a paʎətaɭaɉ tua ɭaku.
S.FORM: sizurung a paljetaladj tua laku.
S.PHON: sizuRuŋ a paʎətaɣaɉ tua ɣaku.
S.TRANSL: She pushed her sister into the well.

S.FORM: qau azua tjavulung a vavayan vaik anga kisan vaɫaw tua qatjuvi.
S.PHON: qau azua cavuɭuŋ a vavajan vaik aŋa kisan vaʎaw tua qacuvi.
S.FORM: qau azua tjavulung a vavayan vaik anga kisan valjaw tua qatjuvi.
S.PHON: qau azua ʦavuɣuŋ a vavajan vaik aŋa kisan vaʎaw tua qaʦuvi.
S.TRANSL: And the older girl went to become the snake’s wife.

S.FORM: manu mangtjez a qatjuvi kasi kavuavuan, sa kiqenetji a vaɫaw.
S.PHON: manu maŋcəz a qacuvi kasi kavuavuan, sa kiqənəci a vaʎaw.
S.FORM: manu mangtjez a qatjuvi kasi kavuavuan, sa kiqenetji a valjaw.
S.PHON: manu maŋʦəz a qaʦuvi kasi kavuavuan, sa kiqənəʦi a vaʎaw.
S.TRANSL: The snake came back from the fields and looked at his wife.

S.FORM: "aku sekulukulung anga a ku vaɫaw?" aya timadju a kinemnem.
S.PHON: "aku səkuɭukuɭuŋ aŋa a ku vaʎaw" aja timaɉu a kinəmnəm.
S.FORM: "aku sekulukulung anga a ku valjaw?" aya timadju a kinemnem.
S.PHON: "aku səkuɣukuɣuŋ aŋa a ku vaʎaw" aja timaɉu a kinəmnəm.
S.TRANSL: “Why has my wife become ugly?” he thought.

S.FORM: manu vaik timadju a tsaɫum a sema tjua laku.
S.PHON: manu vaik timaɉu a ʦaʎum a səma cua ɭaku.
S.FORM: manu vaik timadju a caljum a sema tjua laku.
S.PHON: manu vaik timaɉu a ʦaʎum a səma ʦua ɣaku.
S.TRANSL: And he went to the well to get water.

S.FORM: manu uzai a djurikuku: "hi hi hi hi. manu ku vaɫaw, aya," aya zemaing.
S.PHON: manu uzai a ɉurikuku: "hi hi hi hi. manu ku vaʎaw, aja," aja zəmaiŋ.
S.FORM: manu uzai a djurikuku: "hi hi hi hi. manu ku valjaw, aya," aya zemaing.
S.PHON: manu uzai a ɉuRikuku: "hi hi hi hi. manu ku vaʎaw, aja," aja zəmaiŋ.
S.TRANSL: There was a cockerel there. “Hee hee; ‘She’s my wife’ he thinks,” it crowed.

S.FORM: qau karimen azua djurikuku nua qatjuvi, sa katsu-i a tjumaq.
S.PHON: qau karimən azua ɉurikuku nua qacuvi, sa kaʦu-i a cumaq.
S.FORM: qau karimen azua djurikuku nua qatjuvi, sa kacu-i a tjumaq.
S.PHON: qau kaRimən azua ɉuRikuku nua qaʦuvi, sa kaʦu-i a ʦumaq.
S.TRANSL: The snake looked for the cockerel and took it home.

S.FORM: qau manu vaik azua qatjuvi a sema kavuavuan.
S.PHON: qau manu vaik azua qacuvi a səma kavuavuan.
S.FORM: qau manu vaik azua qatjuvi a sema kavuavuan.
S.PHON: qau manu vaik azua qaʦuvi a səma kavuavuan.
S.TRANSL: And so the snake went off to the fields.

S.FORM: "pagaɫu-i tsu a djurikuku.
S.PHON: "pagaʎu-i ʦu a ɉurikuku.
S.FORM: "pagalju-i cu a djurikuku.
S.PHON: "pagaʎu-i ʦu a ɉuRikuku.
S.TRANSL: “Take care of this cockerel.

S.FORM: maya qemtsi," ayain a vaɫaw.
S.PHON: maja qəmʦi," ajain a vaʎaw.
S.FORM: maya qemci," ayain a valjaw.
S.PHON: maja qəmʦi," ajain a vaʎaw.
S.TRANSL: Don’t kill it,” he said to his wife.

S.FORM: manu ḍemasi tua kasaɫ.
S.PHON: manu ɖəmasi tua kasaʎ.
S.FORM: manu dremasi tua kasalj.
S.PHON: manu ɖəmasi tua kasaʎ.
S.TRANSL: She put some unhulled rice out to dry.

S.FORM: manu tsug a keman azua djurikuku.
S.PHON: manu ʦug a kəman azua ɉurikuku.
S.FORM: manu cug a keman azua djurikuku.
S.PHON: manu ʦug a kəman azua ɉuRikuku.
S.TRANSL: The cockerel kept eating it.

S.FORM: qau limutseng azua vaɫaw; qetsin a djurikuku nazua vaɫaw.
S.PHON: qau ɭimuʦəŋ azua vaʎaw; qəʦin a ɉurikuku nazua vaʎaw.
S.FORM: qau limuceng azua valjaw; qecin a djurikuku nazua valjaw.
S.PHON: qau ɣimuʦəŋ azua vaʎaw; qəʦin a ɉuRikuku nazua vaʎaw.
S.TRANSL: The wife got angry, and she killed the cockerel.

S.FORM: mangtjez azua qatjuvi.
S.PHON: maŋcəz azua qacuvi.
S.FORM: mangtjez azua qatjuvi.
S.PHON: maŋʦəz azua qaʦuvi.
S.TRANSL: The snake came back.

S.FORM: "inu anga ku djurikuku?" ayain a vaɫaw.
S.PHON: "inu aŋa ku ɉurikuku" ajain a vaʎaw.
S.FORM: "inu anga ku djurikuku?" ayain a valjaw.
S.PHON: "inu aŋa ku ɉuRikuku" ajain a vaʎaw.
S.TRANSL: “Where is my cockerel?” he said to his wife.

S.FORM: "keman aravats tua kasaɫ.
S.PHON: "kəman aravaʦ tua kasaʎ.
S.FORM: "keman aravac tua kasalj.
S.PHON: "kəman aRavaʦ tua kasaʎ.
S.TRANSL: “It kept eating the rice,

S.FORM: qau ku qetsin anga," aya kiɫavaran tua vaɫaw a uqaɫay.
S.PHON: qau ku qəʦin aŋa," aja kiʎavaran tua vaʎaw a uqaʎaj.
S.FORM: qau ku qecin anga," aya kiljavaran tua valjaw a uqaljay.
S.PHON: qau ku qəʦin aŋa," aja kiʎavaRan tua vaʎaw a uqaʎaj.
S.TRANSL: so I killed it,” she said to her husband.

S.FORM: qau tsuɫuin anga azua djurikuku.
S.PHON: qau ʦuʎuin aŋa azua ɉurikuku.
S.FORM: qau culjuin anga azua djurikuku.
S.PHON: qau ʦuʎuin aŋa azua ɉuRikuku.
S.TRANSL: So the cockerel was butchered.

S.FORM: nu djemamay a uqaɫay, seti; nu djemamay a vavayan, tsuqelaɫ.
S.PHON: nu ɉəmamaj a uqaʎaj, səti; nu ɉəmamaj a vavajan, ʦuqəɭaʎ.
S.FORM: nu djemamay a uqaljay, seti; nu djemamay a vavayan, cuqelalj.
S.PHON: nu ɉəmamaj a uqaʎaj, səti; nu ɉəmamaj a vavajan, ʦuqəɣaʎ.
S.TRANSL: When they ate it as a side dish, the husband always got meat, and the wife got bones.

S.FORM: qau limutseng azua vavayan; siverits azua djamay.
S.PHON: qau ɭimuʦəŋ azua vavajan; sivəriʦ azua ɉamaj.
S.FORM: qau limuceng azua vavayan; siveric azua djamay.
S.PHON: qau ɣimuʦəŋ azua vavajan; sivəRiʦ azua ɉamaj.
S.TRANSL: The wife was angry, and threw away the side dish.

S.FORM: mintu tsemuvuq a seray itjua vinritsan.
S.PHON: mintu ʦəmuvuq a səraj icua vinriʦan.
S.FORM: mintu cemuvuq a seray itjua vinrican.
S.PHON: mintu ʦəmuvuq a səRaj iʦua vinRiʦan.
S.TRANSL: A pine tree sprouted at the place where the dish landed.

S.FORM: qau meqatsa azua seray, san sangelen.
S.PHON: qau məqaʦa azua səraj, san saŋəɭən.
S.FORM: qau meqaca azua seray, san sangelen.
S.PHON: qau məqaʦa azua səRaj, san saŋəɣən.
S.TRANSL: The pine tree grew tall, and the wood was used for a stool.

S.FORM: nu qemiladj a uqaɫay, minseg.
S.PHON: nu qəmiɭaɉ a uqaʎaj, minsəg.
S.FORM: nu qemiladj a uqaljay, minseg.
S.PHON: nu qəmiɣaɉ a uqaʎaj, minsəg.
S.TRANSL: When the husband sat on it, it was steady;

S.FORM: nu qemiladj a vavayan, maɫeqaɫeqad.
S.PHON: nu qəmiɭaɉ a vavajan, maʎəqaʎəqad.
S.FORM: nu qemiladj a vavayan, maljeqaljeqad.
S.PHON: nu qəmiɣaɉ a vavajan, maʎəqaʎəqad.
S.TRANSL: but when the wife sat on it, it wobbled.

S.FORM: limutseng azua vavayan.
S.PHON: ɭimuʦəŋ azua vavajan.
S.FORM: limuceng azua vavayan.
S.PHON: ɣimuʦəŋ azua vavajan.
S.TRANSL: The wife was angry,

S.FORM: sipetsungu azua sangel.
S.PHON: sipəʦuŋu azua saŋəɭ.
S.FORM: sipecungu azua sangel.
S.PHON: sipəʦuŋu azua saŋəɣ.
S.TRANSL: and used the stool for firewood.
